Hey, welcome back again. You know, there was a little internal debate for me about where in the certification to put this particular content, whether it should be at the end of this week or in the beginning of next week. And really still, I don't know which is better, but I figured we go ahead and put it right here where it is. So if you just watched the training on deadline stacking, one of the questions you might have had was, dude, what do I do if I don't have any cool bonuses or whatever? And as you progress through next week's training, we'll talk about maximizing certain sales processes with order bumps and upsells and stuff like that. And you might start having questions like, dude, what do I do if I don't have any order bumps or upsells or anything like that? And that's what this training actually addresses, which is specifically within the context of information, how to come up with other stuff, whether it's going to be order bumps, upsells, or maybe even things that you can use as a bonus product. So in this training, which you may have seen before, I'm going to walk you through something called an escape and arrival framework. And that framework will do two things for you. Number one, it will give you ideas on what to create that your customers actually want and what they might buy. And it'll also show you how to create it. Pretty simple, super simple, actually. And I think you'll find it handy. So if you're in that situation where you're like, I need bonus stuff, or I need other stuff to sell, or maybe you have a client that's in that situation, then this stuff ought to help you pretty good. Once again, through what is quite clearly Spielberg level editing skills is time for the old Kern editing mastery. Here we go. Three, two, one. So this content is framework content that you've probably seen me teach before. And so what I'm going to do is I'm going to teach it to you, and I'm going to go through it with you as a refresher. But really what I'm giving this to you for is so not that you can use this recording by any means, but so you can have these slides as a means to guide your client through the creation process of more stuff to sell if you need to. So let's talk about upsell products. This is for info. They're usually between 300 or 30 and 500 bucks, although I wouldn't really take that as any sort of gospel truth. I mean, I've got a one cook upsell that's a brand right now that it's working. So go figure. Who knows? They're usually multimedia. They always cover milestones on the escape and arrival framework, and they're always mission specific. So you might understand escape and arrival framework, or maybe you're new to it. Your client won't. So in the first part of this, I'm going to cover what escape and arrival framework is, and then we'll go into the content creation here. So to illustrate it, let's say we're selling to people who want to put their business online. And so that's your client, right? Your client sells how to stick, you know, how to take your flower shop from online to offline or whatever, and they've got something that they're selling. You know, so our first mission is to look at our global milestone list for escape and arrival. And all this means is you take the customer and you say, okay, where is he? What situation does he want to escape from? And what situation does he want to arrive to? And what steps does he have to take in order to get there? And each of those steps is called a milestone. So if we're selling to that business owner who wants to take, you know, they want to take their flower shop online or whatever, they want to escape from a certain situation and they want to arrive to a certain situation. All right? So as usually, you've probably seen me teach this and the results and advanced concepts and everything. What I like to do and what have you do with the client is literally, you know, on a piece of paper or you're doing it over Zoom or you're just going through these slides with them or whatever, but maybe changing them a bit. You say, okay, you know, the person wants to escape from something and he wants to arrive to something and there's probably a series of steps he's got to take. So he wants to escape from not having a website. He wants to arrive to having a website, a know your selling them stuff, Mr. Client. But let's look at every major thing he's got to do in order to actually do that. So the client's like, well, he's got to set up a website and he's got to accept payments on that website and he's got to build like the main pages and then he's got to build like marketing pages. And so you'd be like, Hey, wait a minute, what are you talking about? Well, fundamental pages are going to be like the about us pages, the directions pages and the content pages and all that. And then the marketing pages are going to be the pages that have product descriptions on them and things like that. You're like, okay, that makes sense. And then you got to know what to say on those marketing pages. And incidentally, I'm talking to you now as a consultant, part of your gig might be to get him to clarify this because a lot of times the client doesn't realize the value of what they know. So like that marketing messaging part, they might be like, Yeah, you got to build the marketing pages. And if you don't dig in there and be like, what what constitutes that? How do you know if the marketing page is any good? You might totally miss this milestone if you have to have your messaging right. And so then we'll kind of go on here with each milestone. Well, they've got to automate stuff. And you might be like, What are you talking about? Well, you know, they got to automate like when someone buys something that, you know, they've got to send them the confirmation email, and they got to have the shopping cart, you know, send the thing to the fulfillment house or whatever it is, right? So you got to dig in there. And then they got to get traffic to the website. And then they got to know how to traffic is working. Right. And then finally, there's advanced stuff that they might want to do, you know, like hardcore automation or, you know, intent based branding stuff or whatever it may be. Right. And so this is like kind of all the things that someone with the flower shop down the streets got to go through in order to sell the flowers on the internet. All right. And kind of a nutshell, like big picture. So if we wanted to create upsell products or any products for this person, what we would do is we'd first all of these little vertical lines here, like set up website, fundamental pages, all that. These are called milestones. Right. So we first understand that terminology and that definition. So what we would do is you just take one milestone and you build a standalone product out of it. So let's say that we wanted to create a standalone product called how to set up your website by lunchtime tomorrow. Now remember, set up a website is the first milestone. Right. So we're just building a product just to get them through that one singular milestone. So what we do is we do the same escape and arrival process that illustrates what they have to do from escaping from the situation of not having a website and arriving to the situation of having a website. So you say, okay, Mr. Client, what's a walk me through it? You know, the guy wants to set up a basic web. We're just talking about basic just that one little milestone. What do they got to do? The client says, well, first they got to find the domain, then they got to buy a domain, then they got to host a domain, then they got to set up email, and then they got to like do shit with the domain. Like they're either going to use cPanel and that's going to allow them to set up WordPress or they're going to use a website builder. They got to do something at the very least they've got to have a home page. Right? And so now we've got the bare minimum. So we have here one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, seven steps. All right, they got to do. Okay. So at this stage, now you're with the client, you're like, okay, well, just, you know, and you could be doing this with a legal pad or something while you're talking to them, you're just interviewing them, you say, okay, well, what do they have to do to find a buy a domain? That seems kind of like simple. And they would be like, well, you know, there's a lot of things they could do. Like first of all, usually their domain name is going to be taken and they got to get the right domain. So you go and you can type in a bunch of ideas on GoDaddy, that's called bulk domain search, and usually the good ones that you want are taken. So then you can go to a website like domainspot.com that'll help you come up with ideas for your domain name. You can also go to expireddomains.net and you can come up with ideas there. And then you as the client, you know, like your client will be telling you this, you know, and here's some formulas for choosing domain name. So if you're a Miami Forests, instead of saying like ricksflowersinmiami.com, you might want to do like getflowersinmiami.com or bestmiammieforest.com or something like that. So you're giving them, you know, this is where the client would be sharing their expertise. So if you or I or the client or whatever were to shoot a video on this, just on how to do a bulk domain search on GoDaddy, how to use domains bot for suggestions, how to look for expired domains on expireddomains.net, how to like come up with easy to remember domain name formulas versus the unbelievably bad stuff they're probably going to come up with on their own. If we did that one little piece, that video by itself would probably be 10 to 15 minutes, just that one little thing. Right? And so now they found the domain name, now they got to host it. Well, you and I, like it's mindlessly simple. Like, yeah, dude, just like Google host it and pick one, whatever, Bluehost, GoDaddy, who cares. But most people don't know this, you know, like what is your client's first language is never going to be their their clients first language, their customers first language. So within the choose hosting thing is there's like all kinds of hosting, they're shared hosting, there's dedicated or managed hosting, there's WordPress hosting, and then like all that stuff's got pros and cons. And you and I might be like, Oh my God, that's so unnecessary. But the person who's never done this stuff before is going to be like, they're going to have a billion questions. So you would want to answer all those billion questions. You talk about the pros and cons of each one, how to know which one is right for you. And then a walkthrough of setting up an account for each type or say, Hey, look, you don't need any of this stuff. The easiest thing is shared hosting. Here's how to use Bluehost, or here's how to use GoDaddy, or whatever you want to do. Man, if we shot a video just that, especially if we went through all these different types, I think it'd be as long as 30 minutes. You know, even if we just like didn't show them how to set up all of these different types of hosting, we just said, Okay, here's a different types. Here's the pros and cons of each where I recommend is GoDaddy, here's how to do it. Just that thing by itself, probably be 15, 20 minutes. All right. And then like once you get a domain name, you got a couple choices, right? You can like use C panel, and then you see panel to install like website building things, you know, so if you go down that road, how to find the damn thing, you know, and so if you don't know what C panel is, that's a good sign, right? Because like most people don't. And so if our client was, we were advising the client on how to do this, we would be totally validating that this would be a helpful product to create. So how to find it, overview of the nine million sections of C panel, what you don't want to mess with, so you don't break anything, and how to like get it set up so you can start using some sort of a website builder, that'd probably be 10 minutes, you know, and within C panel, by the way, in most cases, although they do change this constantly, you'd show them how to set up email. So remember, like our product is how to have a working website set up by lunchtime tomorrow, right? It isn't how to build a giant e-commerce, you know, mega site, we're just teaching them the basics. So they're not going to know how to set up Frank at super awesome flowers.biz as an email address. So you got to show them how to create accounts, within their hosting account, email forwarders, you got to show them how to do that. You got to show them how to access and configure webmail if they want to do that, you got to show them how to set up a catchall account, so anything at super awesome, MiamiFloris.biz, this is going to go and get forwarded somewhere, and like, all that stuff needs teaching, because most folks don't know how to do it, you know, so that will probably be a 10 minute video in and of itself. And then we got to talk about building the web page, right? So let's say, you know, you got to have something and let's say you want to teach a WordPress, because it's free, right? So how do you install WordPress? How do you download it? Hey, where do you get WordPress? How do you make sure it's installed properly on your website? And then where do you find the themes? How do you choose a theme? How do you install the damn thing? And then how do you build, you know, the pages in it? That's probably the next video actually. So just these three things, that's probably 15 minutes. And then assuming they're built using WordPress in this case, now the next part is how do you build a homepage? So okay, well, you got to choose the homepage type from the theme. Then you got to edit that page in WordPress, which is horrible, you know, and then you got to set that page is the default homepage of the domain and that in and of itself is probably 15 minutes. So if we were to add all that up, I think that's six total videos. And that's like an hour to 90 minutes of content, right?
